message:
fix(core): make rand/srand thread-safe and harden hex float exponent arithmetic

stdlib/random:
- Replace thread_local Cell<u32> with a global AtomicU32 and use a
  CAS loop for the LCG state transition. The thread-local approach
  meant each thread saw an independent sequence seeded to 1, which is
  incorrect for programs that call srand() on the main thread and
  expect rand() on other threads to see the same seed. The atomic CAS
  loop matches the glibc TYPE_0 LCG behavior where all threads share
  a single global seed.

stdlib/conversion:
- Use saturating arithmetic for hex float fractional digit counter and
  exponent computation to prevent overflow on pathologically long hex
  float literals (e.g., 0x0.00000...001p+2147483647 with millions of
  fractional digits).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
